pattern: To minimize a sum of two numbers built from a fixed multiset of digits, keep every number short by putting each digit in its own tens-or-units slot, and pair the largest digits with the smallest place values.
hints: Since every extra digit you add to a number multiplies its whole value by ten, the cheapest split always uses at most two digits per new number.
Sort the four digits first -- the arrangement that minimizes the sum pairs the two smallest digits as the tens digits and the two largest as the units digits.
With digits sorted ascending as d0 <= d1 <= d2 <= d3, the minimum sum is (d0 + d1) * 10 + (d2 + d3).

edge_inputs.description: all four digits distinct and spread out
leading zero digits allowed in a new number, several zero digits present
three of the four digits are zero

stress.description: randomly sampled four-digit number across the full valid range
another randomly sampled four-digit number, different seed for coverage
